title: Read-only audit in /Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public. Focus on constraints: source naming, bound/unbound Project OS envelope rules, and whether Claude/FUGUE sources would require extra fields or fail validation for GHA backup recording. Return actionable conclusion with file evidence.
summary_text: **Vote:** Add a new success-gated step in `.github/workflows/fugue-codex-implement.yml` that calls `scripts/local/run-kernel-task-completion-backup.sh` directly (with explicit `--assistant/--source/--session-id/--summary`, plus optional `--project-os-*`), not `scripts/lib/kernel-run-complete.sh`. 1. `kernel-run-complete.sh` auto-forces `--no-gha` for non-critical runs (`scripts/lib/kernel-run-complete.sh:100-113`), which creates a local-only/single-lane fallback path in CI and can drop durable backup dispatch. 2. `kernel-run-complete.sh` couples backup to phase-gate + compact lifecycle (`scripts/lib/kernel-run-complete.sh:92`, `143-147`), increasing regression risk in `fugue-codex-implement.yml`, which currently only does manual compact propagation (`.github/workflows/fugue-codex-implement.yml:651-684`). 3. Direct runner path already supports provider-flexible source/assistant and Project OS passthrough in `backup-record` (`scripts/local/run-kernel-task-completion-backup.sh:276-309`, `222-233`) and is covered by existing tests (`tests/test-kernel-task-completion-backup.sh:37-75`).
